source.title: Lacko, Machackova & Smahel — Does violence in video games impact aggression and empathy?
fundingDetails: European Union co-financing: Research of Excellence on Digital Technologies and Wellbeing CZ.02.01.01/00/22_008/0004583

restsOn.ref: NEW:Czech adolescent violent-game longitudinal panel (4 waves; n=3,010)

provenance.quote: Drawing on a representative sample of 3010 Czech adolescents aged 13–17, data were collected over four waves to measure physical and verbal aggression.

position.quote: All desensitization effects were statistically insignificant; the findings challenge violent video games as a significant contributor to heightened aggression in adolescents.

factorWeights.quote: Within-person effects of changes in violent-game exposure on aggression were statistically insignificant.
